entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop being evaluated for resistance to smut disease.

entities.name: SMUT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of the plant to resist infection by smut.

entities.name: FIELD SCREENING TECHNIQUE
entities.description: A developed method to identify smut resistance in pearl millet involving inoculation and scoring.

entities.name: TOLYPOSPORIUM PENICILLARIAE
entities.description: The fungal pathogen used for inoculation to test for smut resistance.

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of injecting a sporidial suspension into the tillers at the boot-leaf stage.

entities.name: HIGH HUMIDITY
entities.description: The environmental state maintained to facilitate the infection process.

entities.name: OVERHEAD SPRINKLER IRRIGATION
entities.description: The method used to maintain high humidity during inoculation and development.

entities.name: PERCENT SEVERITY
entities.description: The metric used to estimate the extent of smut reaction in inflorescences.

entities.name: RAINY SEASON
entities.description: The seasonal period during which the field screening was conducted.
